title: GOOD: Client verifies relay-returned pub_id against locally-computed value
description: After the authenticate request completes, client.rs checks that auth_result["pub_id"] matches identity.pub_id() computed locally. If they differ, the connection is rejected with an explicit error. This catches two failure modes: (1) a misconfigured relay that hashes keys differently, and (2) a relay that impersonates a different user by returning a wrong pub_id in the auth response. The alternative is trusting the relay's returned pub_id, which would let a compromised relay silently reassign the client's identity. The local verification costs one string comparison and closes the attack surface entirely.
